summary: Patch release of postmark-mcp adds install-time execution, broad credential and environment access, undeclared outbound HTTPS, and a runtime behavioral change that BCC-injects every outbound email to an attacker-controlled address. None of these changes are justified by the package purpose or the published changelog.

primary_findings.claim: Candidate version adds a postinstall lifecycle script that did not exist in 1.0.15.
primary_findings.why_it_matters: Install-time code runs automatically during npm install with no user interaction and is the single most common npm supply-chain abuse point.

primary_findings.claim: The new postinstall reads POSTMARK_API_TOKEN, AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID, AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY, and GITHUB_TOKEN from the environment.
primary_findings.why_it_matters: Reading credential-shaped environment variables at install time is inconsistent with an MCP email server and indicates exfiltration intent.

primary_findings.claim: Outbound HTTPS POST to api.attacker.tld is constructed during postinstall and confirmed by DNS trace.
primary_findings.why_it_matters: A package that previously had no install-time network egress now opens an outbound channel to a non-Postmark host on every install.

primary_findings.claim: The sendEmail handler unconditionally adds a BCC recipient to every outbound mail; recipient is hardcoded and not configurable.
primary_findings.why_it_matters: Silent BCC injection routes every email a customer sends through this MCP server to a third party. This is the same pattern as the September 2025 incident writeup.

benign_explanations_considered.hypothesis: The new postinstall is benign telemetry that the maintainer simply forgot to document in the changelog.
benign_explanations_considered.why_rejected: Telemetry would not require reading POSTMARK_API_TOKEN or AWS_* credentials. The capability profile is inconsistent with telemetry.

benign_explanations_considered.hypothesis: The BCC field is part of a new feature that the README and changelog skip discussing.
benign_explanations_considered.why_rejected: A hardcoded non-configurable BCC recipient is not consistent with any user-facing feature. The recipient is not a Postmark-controlled address.

recommended_agent_checks: Confirm api.attacker.tld is not a Postmark-controlled host (whois + ASN check).
Compute the SHA256 of dist/telemetry.js against the maintainer's GitHub releases page to confirm provenance mismatch.
Diff src/handlers/sendEmail.ts against the 1.0.15 baseline inside the audit container to locate the BCC injection.
Check npm publish history: was 1.0.16 published from a different IP or maintainer login than 1.0.15?
developer_safe_summary: ModuleWarden blocked postmark-mcp@1.0.16. The release adds install-time code that reads your environment credentials and silently CC-routes every email through a third party. Pin postmark-mcp@1.0.15 until the maintainer publishes a clean version.
security_admin_summary: BLOCK postmark-mcp@1.0.16 across all consumers. Multi-capability supply-chain compromise: lifecycle script addition + credential reads + undeclared network egress + runtime BCC injection. Notify the Postmark security team; the public maintainer account may be compromised. Last known clean: 1.0.15.
